title: Groq 400 tool_use_failed must not trigger 300s brownout
current_state: Observed live 2026-05-24: in god mode, Ash asked 'can you do browsing?'. Groq (llama-3.3-70b-versatile) returned HTTP 400 with code='tool_use_failed' and failed_generation='<function=web_fetch{"url": "https://www.example.com"}</function>' — the model generated a malformed XML-style tool call instead of proper JSON function-calling format. core/llm_router.py:265-267 catches all exceptions identically and calls _mark_brownout() unconditionally, producing a 300s Groq brownout. Then Ollama (not running) also fails. God mode has no more providers → 'All LLM providers failed or browned out.' The session is blocked from using Groq for 5 minutes because of one bad model generation, not a provider outage.
structural_problem: LLMRouter._mark_brownout() is called on every exception with no error classification. HTTP 4xx errors have two fundamentally different causes: (a) provider outage / rate-limit / auth failure — brownout is correct; (b) model generation quality failure (tool_use_failed, context_length_exceeded, content_policy) — brownout is wrong because the provider is fully healthy. Treating them identically punishes god mode users for a recoverable model error.
target_state: Introduce _is_retryable_generation_error(e) classifier. Errors that are generation-quality failures (400 tool_use_failed, 400 context_length_exceeded) do NOT trigger brownout and instead trigger a single no-tools retry. Only connection errors, 429, 401, 403, 5xx trigger brownout as before.
files_affected: testing/test_llm_router.py (add tests for the new classifier)
audit_refs: Live incident 2026-05-24 — god mode disabled for 5 min after one browse attempt
design_notes: Error classifier: _is_generation_error(e) -> bool — checks str(e) for 'tool_use_failed', 'context_length_exceeded', 'content_policy_violation'; also detects HTTP 400 status on groq/anthropic SDK exceptions
On generation error: do NOT call _mark_brownout(). Log as warning not error. Append to errors list with a 'generation_error' tag.
No-tools retry: if the failed call had tools and got tool_use_failed, retry once with tools=[] (or tools=None). This lets the model answer in plain text instead of trying to call a tool.
If the no-tools retry also fails, THEN treat as a full error (but still do not brownout unless it's a different error class).
Groq SDK exception type: groq.BadRequestError (inherits from groq.APIStatusError). Check e.status_code == 400 and 'tool_use_failed' in str(e).
BROWNOUT_SECS stays 300 — only the classification changes. Existing rate-limit and connection-error brownout behavior unchanged.
Separate log levels: generation errors → WARNING, real provider errors → ERROR (current behavior).

Also fix: the 'All LLM providers failed' RuntimeError message contains raw Groq/Ollama error strings including URLs — this leaks via '[Pi] god router error:' in pi_agent.py. That site should use safe_error (T-104 wiring); note it here as a non-goal for this ticket.
success_criteria: Groq 400 tool_use_failed → no brownout recorded; Groq available immediately for next call
No-tools retry issued when tool_use_failed received with tools in the original request
Groq 429 rate-limit → brownout fires as before (300s)
test_groq_tool_use_failed_no_brownout passes: mock Groq to raise BadRequestError(status_code=400, 'tool_use_failed'); assert brownout dict empty after call
risk_notes: No-tools retry could surface a degraded but successful response (plain text, no tool use). This is strictly better than 'Something went wrong' for 5 minutes. The classifier must not over-match — a Groq 400 with 'invalid_api_key' in message is still a brownout trigger.
migration_plan: 1. Read core/llm_router.py lines 260-272 (the catch block) + understand provider exception types
2. Add _is_generation_error(e: Exception) -> bool classifier function near top of file
3. In chat() catch block: if _is_generation_error(e): log.warning(...), append error, and if tools were passed, issue one no-tools retry before moving to next provider. Do NOT call _mark_brownout.
5. Add tests in testing/test_llm_router.py: tool_use_failed → no brownout, 429 → brownout, retry with tools=None on tool_use_failed
test_plan: test_tool_use_failed_no_brownout — mock provider raises BadRequestError(400, 'tool_use_failed'); call chat(); assert router._brownout is empty
test_tool_use_failed_triggers_no_tools_retry — mock raises on first call (with tools), succeeds on second call (no tools); assert second call attempted with tools=[]
test_rate_limit_still_brownouts — mock raises 429; assert brownout recorded
test_server_error_still_brownouts — mock raises 500; assert brownout recorded
test_generation_error_classifier_does_not_overfire — exception message 'invalid_api_key status_code=400'; assert _is_generation_error returns False
resolution: Added _is_generation_error(exc) classifier to LLMRouter — checks for tool_use_failed, context_length_exceeded, content_policy_violation in exception message. In chat() catch block: generation errors skip _mark_brownout (no 300s penalty), log as WARNING. If tools were passed and failure was tool_use_failed, issues one no-tools retry before moving to next provider. Rate-limit 429 and 5xx still brownout as before. 9 new tests in test_llm_router.py.
